note: President oversees all sectors (regulator-in-chief)
Advises president on macro and sector-level economic policy; no direct regulatory authority but shapes policy across all sectors (15 U.S.C. §1023)

agency: Office of National Drug Control Policy
note: Coordinates federal drug-control policy and oversees pharma/health-system funding related to substance abuse (21 U.S.C. §1701 et seq.)
OCC charters national banks; FinCEN oversees AML; OFAC imposes sanctions; regulates financial services broadly (12 U.S.C. §1 et seq.; 31 U.S.C. §310)
BIS controls dual-use exports; NTIA oversees spectrum; USPTO grants patents; broad mandate over commercial activity (15 U.S.C. §1501 et seq.)
Antitrust Division enforces merger and competition law; FBI/DEA touch financial crime and pharma; broad law-enforcement jurisdiction (28 U.S.C. §501 et seq.)
Largest federal buyer of defense and aerospace systems; DARPA funds dual-use technology (10 U.S.C. §111 et seq.)
Secretary of the Navy oversees naval shipbuilding programs and naval aviation procurement (10 U.S.C. §8013 et seq.)

agency: Department of Health and Human Services
sector: pharmaceuticals, biotech, medical devices, health insurance
note: FDA approves drugs and devices; CMS administers Medicare/Medicaid; NIH funds biomedical R&D (42 U.S.C. §300 et seq.)
CISA protects critical infrastructure; CBP controls border trade; TSA regulates aviation security (6 U.S.C. §101 et seq.)
Sets emission and pollution standards under CAA/CWA/RCRA; affects energy generation, auto manufacturing, and chemical industries (42 U.S.C. §7401 et seq.)
BLM manages federal oil/gas and coal leases; BOEM oversees offshore energy; Reclamation controls water (43 U.S.C. §1331 et seq.)
Regulates nuclear power via NRC coordination; manages Strategic Petroleum Reserve; funds energy R&D (42 U.S.C. §7101 et seq.)
Oversees FAA (aviation safety); NHTSA (auto safety standards); FRA (rail safety); MARAD (maritime) (49 U.S.C. §101 et seq.)
FAA holds exclusive authority over civil aviation safety standards and airspace management (49 U.S.C. §40103)
FHWA funds and oversees the National Highway System; major source of highway construction contracts (23 U.S.C. §101 et seq.)
OSHA sets workplace safety rules; Wage and Hour enforces pay standards; ERISA governs employee benefits; touches all employer sectors (29 U.S.C. §651 et seq.)
Administers Title IV student-loan programs; shapes edtech procurement and higher-education markets (20 U.S.C. §3401 et seq.)

agency: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
note: Coordinates all 18 intelligence-community members; cyber threat analysis affects technology and defense sectors (50 U.S.C. §3023)

agency: Department of Housing and Urban Development
note: FHA insures mortgages; Ginnie Mae guarantees MBS; FHEO enforces fair housing; regulates mortgage markets (42 U.S.C. §3535 et seq.)
ITAR controls arms exports; sanctions affect financial and energy sectors; negotiates bilateral trade agreements (22 U.S.C. §2651 et seq.)

agency: Office of Science and Technology Policy
note: Advises president on R&D priorities; coordinates CHIPS Act implementation; influences federal science funding (42 U.S.C. §6611 et seq.)
Administers Old-Age/Survivors/Disability Insurance (OASDI); major influence on financial-services and insurance markets (42 U.S.C. §901 et seq.)
USDA regulates food safety (FSIS); administers farm lending and commodity support programs; RUS funds rural energy (7 U.S.C. §2201 et seq.)
Manages federal workforce policies and benefits programs; oversees large federal IT contracts for HR systems (5 U.S.C. §1101 et seq.)
Civil space exploration and aeronautics R&D; major procurer of aerospace and advanced-technology contracts (51 U.S.C. §20101 et seq.)
Operates the largest integrated US health system; major buyer of pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and construction services (38 U.S.C. §301 et seq.)
